_comment: Master corridor list. Each corridor has a stable id, plain-language name, the OSM query needed to fetch its centerline, and provenance flags. Sources: ECRC SS4A HIN (FL-AL TPO 2050 LRTP Needs Report Dec 2024), City of Pensacola ATP HIN, FDOT HSIP top segments, original MVP list, $10M SS4A flagship corridors.

corridors.name: Cervantes Street (W) — Pace to A
corridors.summary: Wide road where people cross to reach buses, jobs, and clinics. Crashes here often involve people walking.
corridors.message: Cervantes is the single highest-risk walking corridor downtown. Shorter, protected crossings would help transit riders and neighbors most. Cervantes is a primary walking route to downtown jobs, clinics, and transit. Shorter, signalized crossings are a proven way to reduce pedestrian conflicts here.
corridors.resilience_message: Cervantes is a primary walking route to downtown jobs, clinics, and transit. Shorter, signalized crossings are a proven way to reduce pedestrian conflicts here.

corridors.name: Mobile Highway / US 90 (W of Pine Forest)
corridors.summary: Fast traffic at night near bus stops. Many people walking are hit after dark.
corridors.message: Brighter lights, slower speeds, and safer bus stops would prevent the most crashes per dollar on this stretch. Ranked #2 in the entire 5-county region. Mobile Highway carries workers to west-side industrial jobs and freight toward the port. Lighting and signal upgrades are among the most cost-effective crash countermeasures for a corridor like this.
corridors.treatment: Lower speed limit + lighting + bus stop islands
corridors.resilience_message: Mobile Highway carries workers to west-side industrial jobs and freight toward the port. Lighting and signal upgrades are among the most cost-effective crash countermeasures for a corridor like this.

corridors.name: Nine Mile Road (W) — University to Pensacola Blvd
corridors.summary: Long distances between safe crossings near schools and shopping.
corridors.message: Small islands in the middle of the road give families a safe place to pause when crossing. Top-ranked intersection in Escambia County. Nine Mile Road has the highest crash count in the five-county region. Median refuges and signal upgrades reduce crashes by roughly 41-56% in FHWA evaluations.
corridors.treatment: Median refuges + signal timing for walkers
corridors.resilience_message: Nine Mile Road has the highest crash count in the five-county region. Median refuges and signal upgrades reduce crashes by roughly 41-56% in FHWA evaluations.

corridors.name: Navy Boulevard / US 98
corridors.summary: Workers walking and biking to NAS Pensacola share lanes with fast traffic.
corridors.message: A protected bike lane and clearer crosswalks would help shift workers get to and from base safely. Navy Boulevard is a daily commute route for NAS Pensacola personnel, contractors, and civilians. Protected bike lanes and clearer crossings reduce conflicts for people walking and biking to the base.
corridors.resilience_message: Navy Boulevard is a daily commute route for NAS Pensacola personnel, contractors, and civilians. Protected bike lanes and clearer crossings reduce conflicts for people walking and biking to the base.

corridors.name: Davis Highway / SR 291
corridors.summary: Many driveways and missing sidewalk pieces force people into the street.
corridors.message: Closing sidewalk gaps and tidying driveway access is a low-cost first step. FDOT HSIP top segment. Davis Highway runs through the Cordova retail core. Closing sidewalk gaps and managing driveway access are low-cost first steps.
corridors.resilience_message: Davis Highway runs through the Cordova retail core. Closing sidewalk gaps and managing driveway access are low-cost first steps.

corridors.name: Palafox Street (N) / US 29
corridors.summary: Long urban corridor with mixed pedestrian, transit, and through-traffic conflicts.
corridors.message: Tighter intersections and signal priority for walkers would slow turning cars and make walking to shops safer. Palafox is the spine of historic downtown. Low-cost signal and intersection upgrades reduce conflicts between turning cars and people walking.
corridors.resilience_message: Palafox is the spine of historic downtown. Low-cost signal and intersection upgrades reduce conflicts between turning cars and people walking.

corridors.name: Bayou Boulevard / SR 296
corridors.summary: Busy shopping area with long wait times to cross.
corridors.message: Giving walkers a head start at the signal cuts conflicts with turning cars. FDOT HSIP top segment. Bayou Boulevard serves Cordova Mall and Sacred Heart Hospital. A leading pedestrian interval is a near-zero-cost signal-timing change that reduces turning conflicts.
corridors.resilience_message: Bayou Boulevard serves Cordova Mall and Sacred Heart Hospital. A leading pedestrian interval is a near-zero-cost signal-timing change that reduces turning conflicts.

corridors.name: US 29 / Pensacola Blvd — Cantonment & North
corridors.summary: Long state highway with fatal crashes; flagged as one of the highest fatality corridors in FDOT D3.
corridors.message: Signal coordination and ITS upgrades are the priority fixes identified for this rural-edge corridor. $10M SS4A flagship. US 29 is the freight route to the north county and Alabama. It's one of two Escambia corridors in ECRC's $10,000,000 federal SS4A grant for ITS safety-demonstration improvements (USDOT FY24).
corridors.resilience_message: US 29 is the freight route to the north county and Alabama. It's one of two Escambia corridors in ECRC's $10,000,000 federal SS4A grant for ITS safety-demonstration improvements (USDOT FY24).

corridors.name: Fairfield Drive — Mobile Hwy to Texar
corridors.summary: Broken sidewalks, dim lights, and unsafe bus stops; 941 crashes (FDOT 2018–2022 + FARS fatals 2022–2024), including 11 fatalities.
corridors.message: A $10M federal SS4A grant is funding ITS safety-demonstration improvements on this exact stretch. Fairfield Drive carries workers to Sacred Heart, Baptist, and Cordova retail. ECRC's SS4A-funded crash study (Signal Four Analytics, 2021–2025) counted 1,725 crashes here, including 4 deaths.
corridors.treatment: ITS + lighting + sidewalk + bus stop pads
corridors.resilience_message: Fairfield Drive carries workers to Sacred Heart, Baptist, and Cordova retail. A $10M federal SS4A grant is funding ITS safety-demonstration improvements on this stretch.

corridors.name: Scenic Highway — Summit to Langley
corridors.summary: Drivers speed on a long, straight, scenic road shared with cyclists.
corridors.message: Showing drivers their speed lowers it, and a bike lane gives riders a clear space. Scenic Highway sees frequent lane-departure crashes. Rumble strips and speed feedback are among the cheapest interventions in the FHWA catalog.
corridors.resilience_message: Scenic Highway sees frequent lane-departure crashes. Rumble strips and speed feedback are among the cheapest interventions in the FHWA catalog.

corridors.name: Bayfront Parkway — Tarragona to 17th
corridors.summary: Visitors and locals cross between downtown and the waterfront trail.
corridors.message: Better trail crossings link downtown to the bay for residents and visitors. Bayfront Parkway links downtown and the waterfront for residents and visitors. Improved trail crossings reduce conflicts along the route.
corridors.resilience_message: Bayfront Parkway links downtown and the waterfront for residents and visitors. Improved trail crossings reduce conflicts along the route.

corridors.name: Pace Boulevard / SR 292
corridors.summary: Wide road splitting a residential neighborhood with schools nearby. Pace × Cervantes is the most fatal intersection in Pensacola.
corridors.message: Narrowing lanes and protected crossings would knit a divided neighborhood back together. Lighting study funded 2025. Pace Boulevard divides adjacent neighborhoods. Narrowing lanes and adding protected crossings improve walkability; a lighting study is funded for 2025.
corridors.resilience_message: Pace Boulevard divides adjacent neighborhoods. Narrowing lanes and adding protected crossings improve walkability; a lighting study is funded for 2025.

corridors.name: 9th Avenue / SR 289
corridors.summary: City-designated High Injury Network corridor; mixed residential and commercial.
corridors.message: Speed management and intersection visibility are the levers on 9th Ave. 9th Avenue is the medical-district artery. Speed management and intersection-visibility upgrades support hospital and ambulance access.
corridors.resilience_message: 9th Avenue is the medical-district artery. Speed management and intersection-visibility upgrades support hospital and ambulance access.

corridors.name: Airport Boulevard
corridors.summary: Major east-west connector with shopping, hospitals, and the airport.
corridors.message: Crosswalk visibility and signal timing for walkers near the mall and hospital cluster. Airport Boulevard serves a major hospital-and-retail employment cluster. Crosswalk-visibility and signal-timing fixes reduce conflicts for people walking.
corridors.resilience_message: Airport Boulevard serves a major hospital-and-retail employment cluster. Crosswalk-visibility and signal-timing fixes reduce conflicts for people walking.

corridors.name: Creighton Road
corridors.summary: Residential collector with school access and active transportation gaps.
corridors.message: Sidewalk connectivity and safe school crossings are the priorities here. Creighton Road serves multiple schools. Sidewalk connectivity and safe school crossings are the priorities here.
corridors.resilience_message: Creighton Road serves multiple schools. Sidewalk connectivity and safe school crossings are the priorities here.

corridors.name: Blue Angel Parkway / SR 173
corridors.summary: Suburban arterial with rising crash frequency near growing residential areas.
corridors.message: Roundabouts and access management are common Blue Angel countermeasures. Blue Angel Parkway is a main route to NAS Pensacola. Roundabouts and access management at the worst intersections reduce severe crashes.
corridors.resilience_message: Blue Angel Parkway is a main route to NAS Pensacola. Roundabouts and access management at the worst intersections reduce severe crashes.

corridors.name: Pine Forest Road / SR 297
corridors.summary: Rural arterial with the #2 intersection in the county for crashes.
corridors.message: Intersection treatments at SR 297 / US 90 ALT are top priority. Pine Forest Road is a heavy-truck route to the industrial north. Intersection treatments at SR 297 / US 90 ALT are the top priority.
corridors.resilience_message: Pine Forest Road is a heavy-truck route to the industrial north. Intersection treatments at SR 297 / US 90 ALT are the top priority.

corridors.name: Lillian Highway
corridors.summary: East-west arterial connecting Warrington to Mobile Hwy.
corridors.message: Sidewalk completion and crosswalk visibility on a route with limited safe walking options. Lillian Highway connects west-side neighborhoods to major area employers. Sidewalk completion and crossing upgrades give people safer options on a route with limited walking infrastructure.
corridors.resilience_message: Lillian Highway connects west-side neighborhoods to major area employers. Sidewalk completion and crossing upgrades give people safer options on a route with limited walking infrastructure.
